name: Eddy Duchin
abstract: Edwin Frank Duchin (April 1, 1909 – February 9, 1951), commonly known as Eddy Duchin or alternatively Eddie Duchin, was an American popular music pianist and bandleader during the 1930s and 1940s.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t expect you have any of his albums in your library... But he was a good step leading people to good things because he had marvelous taste... A lot of us wanted to be Eddie Duchin in those days.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: Horace was, uh, he was, in New York he was like, uh, Eddie, he was the second piano for Eddie Duchin. He played piano with Eddie Duchin's orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: And I had Nat Cole and Tatum and Eddie Duchin, and all the pianists of the day at the time
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Duchin made an arrangement on it, made a recording on it.
